superseded_by: failure-disposition-router (2026-07-22, weight 0.96): this item becomes that design's S4 boundary applier. Triage: do NOT build standalone — its race-proven constraints (boundary-only placement after dispatchIteration returns, flock.WithPathLock on inboxDir, per-cycle idempotency stamp, never-lower-weight, claimed-items-untouched) carry over VERBATIM into S4.
title: Chronicle S6: recurrence-driven weight escalation + auto-file, ONLY at the loop iteration boundary (shadow first)
summary: BASE PACKAGE UPDATE 2026-07-11: cycle 661 landed go/internal/recurrence with the Escalator/Autofiler interfaces and EscalationPolicy.Target formula ALREADY DEFINED (unwired). Implement THIS item as the concrete Escalator/Autofiler against those seams - do NOT create a chronicle package. Plan/apply split: recurrence.PlanEscalation(ledger, inboxDir, cfg, cycle) []Action (PURE - ActionBumpWeight{ItemPath, From, To}, ActionFileFixItem{...}) + ApplyEscalation(actions, stage) under flock.WithPathLock(inboxDir/.chronicle.lock). Rules: NON-generic patterns only; Count>=2 && DistinctCycles>=2; bump = min(0.99, base + 0.03*(count-1)); idempotency via a chronicle:{last_bump_cycle:N} stamp INSIDE the item JSON (one bump per recurrence-observation cycle); NEVER lowers a weight; items no longer in inbox/ (claimed into processing/) are NEVER touched; missing-fix auto-file with cause-stable id chronicle-<pattern-slug> (the starvationItemID idiom) at weight 0.8 with injected_by:'chronicle-escalation'. CRITICAL PLACEMENT (race-proven): runs ONLY in cmd_loop at the per-iteration boundary AFTER dispatchIteration has synchronously returned - NO lanes in flight (an escalation atomicwrite racing inboxmover.Claim's os.Rename resurrects a claimed item = double work across fleet lanes). shadow stage writes .evolve/chronicle/escalation-report.json only (review the would-be weight distribution before any write); enforce applies. Compiled default shadow.
acceptance: RED: TestApplyEscalation_IdempotentPerCycleStamp (re-run same cycle = no double bump)
